commons.name: name
commons.what_would_you_like_to_do: What would you like to do?

consent_banner.line1: We and selected third parties use cookies or similar technologies for technical purposes and, with your consent, for measurement. Denying consent may make related features unavailable.
consent_banner.line2: You can freely give, deny, or withdraw your consent at any time.
consent_banner.line3: Use the 'Accept' button to consent to the use of such technologies. Use the 'Reject' button or close this notice to continue without accepting.

settings_modal.AUTH_SESSION_description: Stores user login and session data.
settings_modal._ga_description: Used by Google Analytics to collect data on the number of times a user has visited the website as well as dates for the first and most recent visit.
settings_modal.basic_interactions_description: These trackers are used for LEAF-Writer Commons's basic functionality, including inserting a user's identifier (e.g., GitHub account name or ORCID) into any Web Annotations created. It supports loading, saving, and sharing files through a user's Git repositories. LEAF-Writer Commons can only provide limited functionalities without these cookies.
settings_modal.basic_interactions_description2: If you log in using your GitHub account, GitHub will store several cookies with expiration dates varying from your current session to 1 year. Since the number and names of cookies may change, we invite you to check the
settings_modal.cookie_usage_description: This panel allows you to customize your consent preferences for the usage of third-party services and cookie technology to help us achieve the features and activities described below. You may review and change your choices at any time. Please be aware that denying consent for a particular purpose may make related features unavailable. For more details relative to cookies and other sensitive data, please read the
settings_modal.list of cookies used by GitHub: list of cookies used by GitHub
settings_modal.lw_description: Stores usage data on the browser's local storage: preferred identity and storage providers, preferred language and colour theme, recent repositories and documents, XML schemas, etc.
settings_modal.measurement_description: These cookies collect information about how you use the website, which pages you visit, and which links you click. All the data is anonymized and cannot be used to identify you.
settings_modal.more_information_description: For any queries about our policy on cookies and your choices.
settings_modal.strictly_necessary_description: Necessary cookies are required to make a website usable by enabling basic functions such as page navigation and access to secure areas. The website cannot function properly without these cookies. Third-party error-monitoring service does not use cookies, but is essential for the proper use of this site.

warning.must_accept_cookies_message: You must accept our cookie policies to be able to sign in. Click on the shield icon at the top.
warning.remove_consent_basic_interactions_messag: <0>Removing consent for</0> <1>basic interactions & functionalities</1> <2>will sign you out.</2>

error.message404: You either tried some shady route, or you came here by mistake. Go back to the <1>main page.</1>

error.document_not_supported: The document you are trying to load is not supported. Please check that it has the correct extension and is well-formed.
error.xml_not_well-formed_message: The document you are trying to load is not well-formed. Check that it has the XML extension and follows the correct XML grammar.

desktop.ai_api_bridge_unavailable: Desktop AI API bridge is unavailable.

chinese.detail: This project uses Chinese as its source language. LEAF-Writer can download CBDB (China Biographical Database, ~600 MB), the DILA Buddhist Studies authorities (~85 MB), and Wikidata authority packs for automated tagging. They are stored next to your central entity database.

japanese.detail: This project uses Japanese as its source language. LEAF-Writer can download NDL and Wikidata authority packs for automated tagging. They are stored next to your central entity database.

tibetan.detail: This project uses Tibetan as its source language. LEAF-Writer can download Wikidata authority packs for automated tagging. They are stored next to your central entity database.

database.dont_warn_detach: Don't warn me about detaching again
database.add_alternative_name: Add a name for matching (alternative name)
database.open_project_hint: Open a project to browse its entity database.
database.merge_hint: Select 2+ entities to merge, or click to add an |alternative to the search
database.duplicate_authority_one: Two entities share the same authority id
database.duplicate_authority_many: {{count}} authority ids are shared by several entities
database.lookup_conflict_mismatch: A lookup implied {{authority}} {{value}}, but the entity already carries a different {{authority}} id - verify, or split the entity:
database.merge_dialog_message: Names, authority ids, and descriptions are combined onto the surviving entity. Every tag in every project sharing this database is rewritten to the surviving key. Save open documents first.
database.merge_dialog_note: The selected entry survives; the others are folded into it.
database.keys_updated_summary: {{keysUpdated}} tags updated in {{filesChanged}} files across {{projects}} projects.

dialogs.split_info_message: There is no automatic split: the database cannot know which mentions belong to which of the two people. The reliable way is to delete this entity (tags stay, keys are stripped) and re-run tagging or re-link the mentions — each group to its own new entity.
